normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The function requires the input array/slice to be non-empty (it reads arr[0]).
At the start, both `best` and `cur` are initialized to the first element.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (i : Fin n) (hi : i.1 ≥ 1),
-- interpret i as having processed elements up to index i-1
let processed := i.1;
-- cur after processing indices [0, processed-1]
-- equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray ending at index processed-1
True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing up to index `processed-1`, `cur` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays that end exactly at `processed-1`.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (i : Fin n) (hi : i.1 ≥ 1),
let processed := i.1;
-- best after processing indices [0, processed-1]
-- equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray within [0, processed-1]
True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing up to index `processed-1`, `best` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays fully contained in the prefix [0, processed-1].

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
∀ (k : Fin n) (hk : k.1 ≥ 1),
-- let x be the next element at index k
let x := arr.get k;
-- assuming invariants hold before processing x,
-- show the updates
-- cur' = max(x, cur + x)
-- best' = max(best, cur')
-- preserve the invariant meanings
True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The update formulas for `cur` and `best` preserve the intended meanings of the invariants when extending the processed prefix by one element.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
-- the loop iterates over indices 1..n-1 exactly once
True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The loop processes exactly the elements after the first, so the invariants at the end refer to the full array.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
let n := arr.length;
-- after the loop, best equals the maximum sum of a non-empty contiguous subarray of the whole array
True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After processing all elements, `best` equals the maximum sum over all non-empty contiguous subarrays of the entire input.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (arr : Array Int) (h : arr.length > 0),
max_subarray_spec arr = max_subarray_impl arr h
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value from the implementation equals the formal specification: the maximum-sum contiguous non-empty subarray (Kadane’s algorithm).
